lede: A look at October 31 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Leonor, Princess of Asturias and Justin Chatwin, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2017 New York City truck attack
events.desc: A truck drives into a crowd in Lower Manhattan, New York City, killing eight people.

events.title: Mahathir Mohamad
events.desc: Mahathir Mohamad resigns as Prime Minister of Malaysia and is replaced by Deputy Prime Minister Abdullah Ahmad Badawi, marking an end to Mahathir's 22 years in power.

events.title: Singapore Airlines Flight 006
events.desc: Singapore Airlines Flight 006 crashes on takeoff from Taipei, killing 83.

events.title: EgyptAir Flight 990
events.desc: EgyptAir Flight 990 crashes into the Atlantic Ocean near Nantucket, killing all 217 people on board.

events.title: Indira Gandhi
events.desc: Indian Prime Minister Indira Gandhi is assassinated by two Sikh security guards. Riots break out in New Delhi and other cities and around 3,000 Sikhs are killed.

events.title: Provisional Irish Republican Army
events.desc: Three Provisional Irish Republican Army members escape from Mountjoy Prison, Dublin aboard a hijacked helicopter that landed in the exercise yard.

events.title: 1963 Indiana State Fairgrounds Coliseum gas explosion
events.desc: Indiana State Fairgrounds Coliseum gas explosion: A gas explosion at the Indiana State Fairgrounds Coliseum in Indianapolis kills 81 people and injures another 400 during an ice show.

events.title: USS Reuben James (DD-245)
events.desc: World War II: The destroyer USS Reuben James is torpedoed by a German U-boat near Iceland, killing more than 100 U.S. Navy sailors. It is the first U.S. Navy vessel sunk by enemy action in WWII.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: Battle of Beersheba: The "last successful cavalry charge in history".

events.title: Romulus Augustulus
events.desc: Romulus Augustulus is proclaimed Western Roman Emperor.

deaths.text: Ted Sorensen, American lawyer, 8th White House Counsel
Indira Gandhi, Indian politician, Prime Minister of India
H. L. Davis, American author and poet
John Lynch (1740–1820), American city founder and abolitionist

observances.text: Christian feast day: Erc of Slane (in Cornwall)
Christian feast day: Paul Shinji Sasaki and Philip Lindel Tsen (Episcopal Church)
